steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined claim states that My Name Is Red juxtaposes literary realism with Turkish folklore. The correct quotation must show both elements actively present together — not just that one influenced the other, but that Turkish folklore intrudes into an otherwise realistic plot.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Turkish folklore clearly informs the style … but the novel also shows influence of magical realism.': credits both Turkish folklore and Latin American magical realism as influences, but doesn’t show the two elements (realism + folklore) in direct tension within the novel itself. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The logic of the realistic plot is repeatedly and productively disrupted by imagery and situations drawn from Turkish folklore.': directly demonstrates juxtaposition — a realistic plot disrupted by Turkish folklore. This matches the claim precisely. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Like magical realist works, the novel is indebted to antirealistic elements in Turkish folkloric tradition.': frames folklore as a debt or influence rather than showing it actively disrupting the realistic mode; doesn’t capture the live tension of juxtaposition. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The novel alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes … Turkish folklore nevertheless occurs throughout.': mentions alternation rather than juxtaposition, and the “nevertheless” framing weakens the connection between folklore and the realistic plot. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
